ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for raw skinless duck meat; SNOMED CT coverage of raw poultry sub-types is limited.

serving.description: 1 serving (approx. 100 g raw duck without skin)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical raw serving of skinless duck is approximately 100 g, representing a single portion of breast, leg, or wing meat before cooking.

nutrient_source.title: Duck, domesticated, meat only, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171478)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for domesticated duck, meat only (skin removed), raw (FDC ID 171478) used as the primary approved nutrient source. Core macros (energy 135 kcal, protein 18.28 g, fat 5.95 g, carbohydrate 0.94 g), sodium 74 mg, potassium 271 mg, calcium 11 mg, iron 2.4 mg, and cholesterol 77 mg are consistent with the Singapore HPB Foodo database entry (data_source: hpb, confidence 0.95); HPB values adopted where both sources agree. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, B-vitamins, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, and water_g sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 171478 where HPB did not report these fields.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — food contains negligible carbohydrates
gi_source.source_match_notes: Raw skinless duck meat contains only 0.94 g carbohydrate per 100 g. The University of Sydney GI Database does not assign a GI value to foods with negligible carbohydrate content, as GI testing requires a minimum carbohydrate load. Glycemic index is not applicable for this food; glycemic_index is set to null and gi_category to 'not_applicable' per Veritas protocol for low/zero-carbohydrate foods. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate contribution is negligible.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (74 mg/100 g) with a favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio (271 mg K : 74 mg Na)
hypertension.guidance: Raw skinless duck is a low-sodium protein source suitable for hypertensive individuals; avoid high-sodium marinades, glazes, or braising sauces such as soy sauce, hoisin, or oyster sauce, and prefer steaming or roasting without added salt to preserve the naturally low sodium content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate (0.94 g/100 g) with zero sugar; high protein (18.28 g/100 g) supports glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: Raw skinless duck has negligible carbohydrate and zero sugar, making it an appropriate protein choice for people with type 2 diabetes; pair with low-GI vegetables and whole grains, and avoid sweet glazes or marinades containing plum sauce, honey, or hoisin that would add significant sugar load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate total fat (5.95 g/100 g) with saturated fat at 1.83 g/100 g; predominantly unsaturated fat profile (monounsaturated 2.19 g, polyunsaturated 0.77 g); cholesterol 77 mg/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Skinless raw duck has a relatively favourable fat profile with saturated fat well below the 5 g/100 g threshold; it can be included in a heart-healthy diet when prepared by steaming, poaching, or roasting without added saturated fats, and consumed in moderate portions as part of a diet rich in vegetables, legumes, and whole grains.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: No omega-3 fatty acids or anti-inflammatory micronutrients; lean protein and iron support tissue repair and anaemia of chronic disease
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Raw skinless duck provides lean protein and iron beneficial for RA patients with anaemia of chronic disease, but lacks omega-3 fatty acids and anti-inflammatory compounds; prepare by steaming or poaching to minimise advanced glycation end-product (AGE) formation, and pair with omega-3-rich foods such as oily fish for comprehensive RA management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Duck, whole, raw, without skin is the uncooked flesh of domesticated duck (Anas platyrhynchos domesticus) with the skin fully removed, encompassing breast, leg, thigh, and wing portions. The meat displays a deep red colour characteristic of waterfowl, reflecting a higher myoglobin content than chicken, and has a firm, dense texture that becomes tender and richly flavoured when cooked. Without the skin, the fat content drops substantially to approximately 5.95 g per 100 g, with saturated fat at only 1.83 g per 100 g, making it a leaner option than skin-on duck. It is an excellent source of complete protein at 18.28 g per 100 g, supporting muscle maintenance and tissue repair. Iron content is notable at 2.4 mg per 100 g, relevant for individuals with iron-deficiency anaemia or rheumatoid arthritis-related anaemia of chronic disease. Sodium is very low at 74 mg per 100 g, and potassium is 271 mg per 100 g, yielding a favourable ratio for blood pressure management. Carbohydrate is negligible at 0.94 g per 100 g with zero sugar, making this food appropriate for low-carbohydrate, ketogenic, and diabetic dietary patterns. Clinically, skinless raw duck is suitable for individuals managing hypertension, type 2 diabetes, and cardiovascular disease when prepared without high-sodium or high-sugar condiments. It is used globally in roasting, braising, steaming, and confit preparations, and features prominently in Chinese, French, and Southeast Asian cuisines.

meal_context: meal prep base for duck dishes

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171478) for domesticated duck, meat only, raw — an approved primary source. Values are consistent with the Singapore HPB Foodo database entry (confidence 0.95). Both approved sources are in close agreement on core macros and key micronutrients. Fatty acid breakdown and selected micronutrients supplemented from USDA SR Legacy where HPB did not report them.
notes: Dietary fibre is 0.0 g/100 g: confirmed zero — duck meat is an animal product containing no plant cell-wall material; naturally zero. Sugar is 0.0 g/100 g: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 171478 and HPB Foodo. Trans fat is 0.0 g/100 g: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy; no industrially produced trans fat in raw duck meat and ruminant trans fat is not applicable to poultry. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.94 g/100 g); glycemic_index set to null per Veritas protocol for low/zero-carbohydrate foods. Fields set to -1 (data not available in USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 171478 or HPB Foodo): vitamin_c_mg — not reported in either source for this entry; vitamin_a_mcg_rae — not reported in HPB; folate_mcg_dfe — not reported in HPB; vitamin_d_mcg — not reported in HPB; vitamin_e_mg — not reported in HPB; selenium_mcg — not reported in HPB. B-vitamins (B1 0.18 mg, B2 0.26 mg, B3 4.78 mg, B6 0.25 mg, B12 0.4 mcg), phosphorus 203 mg, magnesium 19 mg, zinc 1.87 mg, and water_g 73.8 sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 171478 as HPB did not report these fields. Halal status is not confirmed as halal certification depends on slaughter method; is_halal set to false pending certification confirmation. This record covers the raw, skinless whole duck as a generic ingredient; cooked preparations (roasted, braised, confit) should be recorded as separate entries.
